$comment: Consolidated do-not-translate glossary for spec 101. Seeded from two sources: (1) the enforced terms in commit ed03141e on branch translations-NL (common.en.json / common.nl.json keys), and (2) the Initial Glossary Draft in spec.md. The commit is authoritative for what is actually enforced today; the spec draft contributed rationales, the de translations, and the locked/preferred status distinction. Provenance is recorded per entry in `source`. Schema follows spec.md F1 + Key Entities.

glossary.rationale: Product branding; core organizational unit. Must remain unchanged across all languages. Was being translated to 'Ruimte' in NL.
Plural of Space; product branding consistency. Was being translated to 'Ruimtes' in NL.
Product branding; nested organizational unit. Was being translated to 'Subruimte' in NL. (Missing from spec.md Initial Glossary Draft — added from the commit.)
Product branding; core organizational unit. Must remain unchanged across all languages. Was being translated to 'Subruimtes' in NL.
Product branding; content creation unit. Avoid translations like 'Bericht', 'artikel' or 'Beitrag'. Was being translated to 'Bericht' in NL.
Plural of Post; product branding consistency. Was being translated to 'Berichten' in NL.
Legacy/data-layer term mapped to 'Post' for consistency. Users see 'Post' across the UI. Was being translated to 'Oproep' in NL.
Legacy/data-layer plural mapped to 'Posts'. Was being translated to 'Oproepen' in NL.
Technical term; avoid localized alternatives like 'sjabloon', 'Vorlage', 'plantilla'. Was being translated to 'sjabloon' in NL.
glossary.notes: Stored lowercase in common.* for natural mid-sentence interpolation; capitalize ('Template') when standalone as a tab title, dialog heading, or button label.

glossary.rationale: Plural technical term; maintain consistency with singular. Was being translated to 'sjablonen' in NL.
glossary.notes: Lowercase in common.*; capitalize ('Templates') when standalone.

glossary.rationale: Product feature term; UX consistency across languages. Was being translated to 'Indeling' in NL.
Product feature branding; AI agent role. Must remain consistent. Was being translated to 'Virtuele bijdrager' in NL.
Plural product feature branding. Was being translated to 'Virtuele bijdragers' in NL.

Platform role term — the organisation or person responsible for a Space. Keep in English; avoid localized alternatives like 'Gastheer' (NL), 'Gastgeber' (DE), 'Anfitrión' (ES), 'Hôte' (FR), 'Домакин' (BG). Currently enforced for NL only.
glossary.notes: Lowercase mid-sentence (e.g. 'Contact opnemen met de host'); capitalize ('Host') when standalone as a label, heading, or button.
